summary: No transcript selection strategy configured. BQSR skipped without documented justification. Minor annotation database version concern. No critical pipeline failures detected.

findings.title: No transcript selection strategy configured for VEP annotation
findings.message: VEP runs without --mane_select, --canonical, or --pick. Default returns all transcripts for each variant, and downstream processing selects by longest transcript heuristic. Pathogenic splice variants may be annotated against non-MANE transcripts, reporting them as intronic when the MANE transcript annotation would be coding.
findings.impact: Potentially missed pathogenic variants at exon boundaries for non-dominant transcripts. Protein generation may use non-canonical isoforms.

findings.title: BQSR skipped without documented justification
findings.message: Base quality score recalibration (BQSR) is absent from the alignment pipeline. No comment or config parameter explaining the omission. For WGS data from Illumina sequencers, systematic base quality errors introduce a 2-5% false positive rate increase at Q20 quality boundaries.
findings.impact: Systematic base quality errors propagate to variant quality scores. Modest but consistent false positive rate increase across the callset.
findings.recommendation: Add GATK BaseRecalibrator → ApplyBQSR with --known-sites matching reference build. If deliberately omitted (e.g., for DeepVariant which performs internal recalibration), document the reason in a config comment.
findings.code_snippet:
// alignment.nf: BWA-MEM2 | samtools sort -> HaplotypeCaller
// No BaseRecalibrator or ApplyBQSR step

findings.title: gnomAD v3.1.2 used with GRCh38 pipeline — v4.0 now available
findings.message: Annotation config specifies gnomAD v3.1.2 for population frequency filtering. gnomAD v4.0 was released in late 2023 and includes ~30% more samples plus additional population strata. Variants reclassified between v3.1.2 and v4.0 may affect pathogenicity assessments.
findings.impact: Minor: some variants classified as rare in v3.1.2 may be reclassified as common in v4.0 (true positives filtered) or vice versa. No correctness impact for established variants.
findings.recommendation: Consider upgrading to gnomAD v4.0 for new analyses. For ongoing studies, document the gnomAD version in Methods to ensure reproducibility.
